_comment: Workshop Intro Chapter 05 — Screenshots & Typora Markdown Workflow. Single-stem rendering: scenes joined with break tags, one ElevenLabs call. Visual concerns (sourceStart, clipDurationSeconds, label, title) live in src/WorkshopIntroCh05.tsx. Source: workshop-intro-chapters.mp4 chapter 05 (495.1-681.0s). House voice preset.

scenes.text: Screenshots into Claude — take one with your usual shortcut, then hold shift while you drag it in. That pastes it inline as part of the conversation.
For reading the markdown Claude generates, install Typora. Fourteen bucks, seven-day trial. The cleanest markdown reader for this workflow.
Raw markdown is dense — Typora renders it as a clean document. Headings, lists, tables, all properly typeset. Your CLAUDE-dot-md becomes something you'd actually read.
Typora ships with themes — light, dark, GitHub, Night. Pick the one that matches your screen and stick with it.

Where Typora really earns its keep — rendering Mermaid diagrams inline. When you're mapping automations in Claude, ask it to write the diagram in Mermaid. Typora turns it into a real visual you can scan.
